title: finance: aws provider reads seed balance only, no decay
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Drops the daily decay logic in aws.mjs — ~95% of spend on this account is Claude via Marketplace which credits don't absorb, so the auto-decrement produced wrong balance projections.
- New flow matches the Azure pattern: \`current_balance_usd\` comes from \`secrets/aws-credits.json\` (manual monthly seed from the AWS Billing Console), and \`mtd_cash_usd\` is the live Cost Explorer UnblendedCost so the orchestrator injects it into next month's column where the Automat-IT Wise wire will settle it.
- Documents the new Myceli AWS Organization (8 Control Tower accounts, payer is Automat-IT) and the SSO incognito workaround in the provider-billing playbook.

Operator ritual is now: open the Console Credits tab once a month, copy "Total amount remaining" into \`balance_usd\`, bump \`as_of\`.

Local-only files (gitignored):
- \`apps/operation/finance/secrets/aws-credits.json\` — seeded with the current $32,142.31 balance
- \`apps/operation/finance/secrets/vendors.json\` — added \`AUTOMAT-IT MALAM TEAM SPAIN SLU\` → canonical \`AWS\` (forecast \`"none"\`), updated OpenAI counterparties to \`forecast: "last"\`

gist.headline: Balancing the AWS Books
gist.blurb: Refactored our internal AWS billing tracker to handle Claude Marketplace spend more accurately. No more phantom credit decay!
gist.summary: Updated the internal AWS billing provider to read a manual monthly seed balance instead of using daily decay logic, as Claude Marketplace spend bypasses AWS credits.
gist.impact: Internal finance projections for AWS and Automat-IT are now accurate, requiring only a once-a-month manual balance update.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot acting as an accountant, looking at a ledger or a glowing cloud server, with the Nomnom creature eating old receipts to represent dropping the old decay logic.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing tiny reading glasses, reviewing a glowing digital ledger next to a chunky retro server rack. The round tan nomnom creature happily eats a stack of old paper receipts. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, emotionally warm.
